county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Cadiz. Formed 1820 from Christian and Caldwell. Western Kentucky. Trigg County Historical Society. Land Between the Lakes area — TVA (Kentucky Lake, Lake Barkley) displaced many families; relocation records important. Cadiz community records. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Christian and Caldwell counties for pre-1820 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1820 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1820. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Trigg County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1820.

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.

Tax lists naming slaveholders and number of enslaved; estate inventories naming enslaved individuals; slave schedules (1850, 1860); manumission records; bills of sale. Kentucky was a slave state that did not secede.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Tax lists and estate records generally well preserved

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Bedford. Formed 1837 from Gallatin, Henry, and Oldham. Northern Kentucky. Trimble County Historical Society. Ohio River county. Small agricultural community. FamilySearch microfilm. Check parent counties for pre-1837 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1837 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1837. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Trimble County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1837.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Morganfield. Formed 1811 from Henderson. Western Kentucky. Union County Historical Society. Camp Breckinridge (WWII military camp, 1942-1962) records. Morganfield city records. Ohio River heritage. Coal mining in portions. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Henderson County for pre-1811 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1811 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1811. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Union County Coal Mining and Company Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Western Kentucky coalfield. Company town records, mine payrolls, commissary records, and United Mine Workers union records. Essential for documenting mining families.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Bowling Green. Formed 1796 from Logan. South-central Kentucky. Warren County Historical Society. Western Kentucky University Library Special Collections (outstanding regional genealogy resource — Manuscripts & Folklife Archives). Bowling Green is KY's third-largest city with city directories from 1870s. Bowling Green was provisional Confederate capital of Kentucky. National Corvette Museum has GM plant worker records. Riverview at Hobson Grove (Civil War). Lost River Cave. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1796 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1796. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Warren County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1796.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Tax lists and estate records generally well preserved

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Kentucky was a border state — both Union and Confederate service records. Union pension files at NARA; Confederate pension files at KDLA. Compiled Military Service Records for both sides. USCT (United States Colored Troops) records for African American soldiers.
Annual tax lists enumerate heads of household, land acreage, town lots, horses, cattle, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Essential substitute when other records destroyed.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Springfield. Formed 1792 at statehood from Nelson. Central Kentucky. Washington County Historical Society. Springfield is one of Kentucky's oldest towns. Lincoln Homestead State Park (Thomas Lincoln heritage). Strong Catholic heritage — church records essential. Holy Cross parish records. FamilySearch microfilm. Parent county for Marion.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1792 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1792. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1792.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Monticello. Formed 1800 from Cumberland and Pulaski. South-central Kentucky near Tennessee line. Wayne County Historical Society. Lake Cumberland extends into Wayne County. Mill Springs Civil War battlefield (Battle of Logan's Cross Roads, 1862) records at NPS and NARA. Monticello community records. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1800 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1800. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1800.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Dixon. Formed 1860 from Henderson, Hopkins, and Union. Western Kentucky. Webster County Historical Society. Dixon and Providence community records. Coal mining records. Tradewater River heritage. FamilySearch microfilm. Check parent counties for pre-1860 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1860 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1860. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Williamsburg. Formed 1818 from Knox. Southeastern Kentucky. Whitley County Historical Society. University of the Cumberlands (1889) archives. Cumberland Falls State Resort Park. Williamsburg is a regional center. Coal mining records from late 1800s. Strong Unionist county in Civil War. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Knox County for pre-1818 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1818 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1818. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

Kentucky was a border state — both Union and Confederate service records. Union pension files at NARA; Confederate pension files at KDLA. Compiled Military Service Records for both sides. USCT (United States Colored Troops) records for African American soldiers.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1860 from Breathitt, Morgan, Owsley, Powell.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Campton. Formed 1860 from Breathitt, Morgan, Owsley, and Powell. Eastern Kentucky mountains. Wolfe County Historical Society. Red River Gorge (Natural Bridge). Lee's Junior College had local history materials. Swift Silver Mine legend connections. Lumber industry records. FamilySearch microfilm. Check four parent counties for pre-1860 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1860 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1860. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Versailles. One of Kentucky's original counties from Virginia era (1788). Inner Bluegrass region. Woodford County Historical Society. Versailles is a center of Bluegrass horse culture. Pisgah Church records (oldest west of Alleghenies, 1784). Jack Jouett house. Buckley Wildlife Sanctuary area. Significant enslaved persons records — large plantations. Pre-statehood records at LVA. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1788 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1788. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1788.

source_profiles.source_name: Virginia Land Grants — Woodford County Area
source_profiles.repository: Library of Virginia, Richmond / KDLA, Frankfort
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-statehood Virginia land grants, surveys, and patents for Kentucky District. Virginia military bounty warrants (south of Green River). Kentucky was part of Virginia until 1 June 1792.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well preserved at LVA and KDLA
